# rules.md
# GSAP Animated Website — IDE Agent Rules
# Theme: Dark Formula A / Blue Accent — Aurora = celestial void, near-void deep space, electric aurora light
# Color formula used: Formula A: Neutral Dark + Blue Accent (#4F7EFF), secondary teal (#4FBFBF) for aurora glow
# Generated by projectGRID gsap-website skill

---

## SETUP — Run this first, before any file is created

```bash
npx skills add https://github.com/greensock/gsap-skills
```

---

## Library Stack — Copy-paste into every HTML file before </body>

```html
<!-- Lenis smooth scroll -->
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@studio-freight/lenis@1.0.42/dist/lenis.min.js"></script>

<!-- GSAP core + plugins -->
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.5/gsap.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.5/ScrollTrigger.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.5/SplitText.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.5/MorphSVGPlugin.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.5/DrawSVGPlugin.min.js"></script>

<!-- Three.js — for the aurora particle canvas in the hero -->
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/three.js/r128/three.min.js"></script>

<!-- Barba.js page transitions -->
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@barba/core@2.9.7/dist/barba.umd.js"></script>
```

Initialize in this exact order:
```js
gsap.config({ nullTargetWarn: false });
gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger, SplitText, MorphSVGPlugin, DrawSVGPlugin);
ScrollTrigger.normalizeScroll(true);

const lenis = new Lenis();
lenis.on('scroll', ScrollTrigger.update);
gsap.ticker.add((time) => lenis.raf(time * 1000));
gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0);

// Then: custom cursor, then aurora particle canvas, then Barba, then section ScrollTrigger scenes
```

---

## Project Brief

**Site purpose:** Aurora Global Hackathon 2025 — event landing page and participant funnel. Drives idea submissions, communicates event structure, and converts sponsors and participants. Hosted in association with IIIT Delhi. 7,000-15,000 expected participants from 50+ countries.
**Sections:** Hero (full-viewport, particle aurora), Live Countdown, About/Mission, Event Timeline (5 stages), Award Tracks, Incubation Program, Judges/Mentors, Partners, Register CTA / Footer
**Key CTAs:** "Submit Your Idea" (primary), "Become a Partner" (secondary), "Explore the Timeline" (tertiary scroll-trigger)
**Tone:** Cinematic, authoritative, urgent, cosmically scaled. Not playful. Not startup-casual. This is institutional weight fused with extreme visual energy.

---

## Color System

All values as CSS custom properties. No hardcoded hex values anywhere in the codebase except inside :root.
The palette follows the 60-30-10 rule: 60% dominant surfaces, 30% secondary structure, 10% accent.
One primary accent (blue). One supporting secondary accent (teal) for aurora atmospheric effects only.
All text/background pairs verified at WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text).

```css
/* Font imports — place in <head> */
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Space+Grotesk:wght@400;700&family=Inter:wght@300;400&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
/* Note: Use Clash Display if available via CDN or local. Fallback chain below covers this. */

:root {
  /* ── 60% Dominant surfaces ─────────────────── */
  --color-bg:        #080810;   /* near-void, slight blue undertone — NOT pure black */
  --color-surface-1: #0F0F1A;   /* cards, panels — Level 1 elevation */
  --color-surface-2: #16162A;   /* hover states — Level 2 elevation */
  --color-surface-3: #1E1E36;   /* modals, overlays — Level 3 elevation */

  /* ── 30% Secondary / structure ─────────────── */
  --color-border:    #1C1C30;   /* subtle separators */
  --color-border-2:  #28283E;   /* visible dividers */
  --color-muted:     #5A5A7A;   /* secondary text, labels — 3.1:1 on bg, AA large */
  --color-subtle:    #2A2A42;   /* de-emphasized backgrounds */

  /* ── 10% Accent (primary) ────────────────────── */
  --color-accent:     #4F7EFF;   /* electric blue, ~70% sat — NOT #0000FF */
  --color-accent-dim: #4F7EFF28; /* accent at 16% opacity for bg washes */
  --color-accent-glow:#4F7EFF44; /* accent at 27% opacity for glow/halos */

  /* ── Secondary accent — aurora atmospheric only ─ */
  --color-accent-2:     #4FBFBF;  /* teal, ~60% sat — split-complementary to blue, 15% less saturated */
  --color-accent-2-dim: #4FBFBF20; /* teal at 12% opacity — for aurora shimmer overlays only */
  /* USAGE RULE: --color-accent-2 is ONLY for the aurora particle canvas, section divider glows,
     and the Incubation Program section teal line. Never use on text. Never use on buttons. */

  /* ── Text ────────────────────────────────────── */
  --color-text:   #EEEDF4;   /* primary — 15:1+ on bg */
  --color-text-2: #AAAABF;   /* secondary — 6:1 on bg, AA */
  --color-text-3: #5A5A7A;   /* tertiary — 3.1:1 on bg, use 20px+ labels only */

  /* ── Typography ──────────────────────────────── */
  --font-display: 'Space Grotesk', 'Clash Display', system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-body:    'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
  --font-mono:    'JetBrains Mono', monospace;

  /* ── Type scale — fluid clamp, no fixed px ───── */
  --text-xs:   clamp(0.70rem,  0.9vw,  0.85rem);
  --text-sm:   clamp(0.85rem,  1.1vw,  1.00rem);
  --text-base: clamp(1.00rem,  1.4vw,  1.10rem);
  --text-lg:   clamp(1.10rem,  1.8vw,  1.30rem);
  --text-xl:   clamp(1.40rem,  2.5vw,  1.90rem);
  --text-2xl:  clamp(1.90rem,  4.0vw,  3.20rem);
  --text-3xl:  clamp(2.80rem,  7.0vw,  5.50rem);
  --text-hero: clamp(5.00rem, 13.0vw, 12.00rem);

  /* ── Spacing — 8pt grid ──────────────────────── */
  --space-xs:  clamp(0.50rem, 1.0vw, 0.75rem);   /* ~8px  */
  --space-sm:  clamp(0.75rem, 1.4vw, 1.00rem);   /* ~16px */
  --space-md:  clamp(1.00rem, 2.0vw, 1.50rem);   /* ~24px */
  --space-lg:  clamp(1.50rem, 3.0vw, 2.50rem);   /* ~32-40px */
  --space-xl:  clamp(2.50rem, 5.0vw, 4.00rem);   /* ~48px */
  --space-2xl: clamp(4.00rem, 7.0vw, 6.00rem);   /* ~64-96px */
  --space-3xl: clamp(6.00rem, 10vw,  9.00rem);   /* ~96-128px */

  /* ── Radii ───────────────────────────────────── */
  --radius-sm:   3px;
  --radius-md:   8px;
  --radius-lg:   16px;
  --radius-pill: 100vw;

  /* ── Easing references ───────────────────────── */
  --ease-out-expo:     cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
  --ease-in-out-quart: cubic-bezier(0.76, 0, 0.24, 1);
}
```

Typography enforcement:
- Display text: var(--font-display), weight 700 to 800, letter-spacing -0.04em
- Body text: var(--font-body), weight 300 or 400 only, line-height 1.70
- Mono text: var(--font-mono), weight 400, letter-spacing +0.10em, uppercase only
- All body text containers: max-width 68ch enforced
- Line-height on display: 0.92 to 1.00
- Hero "AURORA" title specifically: letter-spacing -0.06em, line-height 0.88, weight 800

Spacing enforcement:
- All spacing is 8pt multiples. Use the custom properties above.
- Optical correction allowed: uppercase pill padding (-1px top), letter-spaced mono (+0.5em right), icon baseline nudge (3px), SplitText descender clip (padding-bottom 0.15em).
- No other non-8pt values anywhere.

---

## Layout Principles

The nav is a fixed horizontal strip — but it is NOT centered links. It is a single-line layout with "AURORA" wordmark flush left in var(--font-mono) uppercase, and a single accent-colored "SUBMIT IDEA" pill button magnetic-pulled flush right. No nav links. No hamburger. The site is single-page scrolling — navigation is purely contextual scroll anchors triggered by the section indicators.

Section indicators: a vertical strip of 8 numbered mono labels on the right edge of the viewport, fixed position, always visible. Each label is the section number in var(--font-mono) var(--color-muted). The active section indicator animates: its number scales 1.4x, color transitions to var(--color-accent), and a horizontal accent line draws in from left using DrawSVGPlugin over 0.4s ease: "expo.out".

Hero: "AURORA" spans the full viewport width in a single unbroken word. Letters are clipped by overflow: hidden on per-char spans. The word BLEEDS off the right edge intentionally — the "A" at the end is cut by the viewport. This is the structural statement: Aurora is too large to contain.

The Event Timeline section is structurally inverted: it is a horizontal scroll track pinned to the viewport while the user scrolls 500vh vertically. The 5 stages move right-to-left like a film reel. Each stage card is 60vw wide minimum. The stage number is displayed in var(--text-hero) opacity: 0.05 as a background watermark behind the card content.

The Tracks/Awards section uses a 2x2 asymmetric grid: top row is one 60% card + one 40% card. Bottom row reverses: 40% + 60%. Cards have zero border-radius. This prevents the standard equal-three-column layout entirely.

The Partners section is a continuous looping horizontal marquee — not a static logo grid. Logos scroll left infinitely. On hover of any logo, the entire marquee pauses (lenis.stop()) and the hovered logo scales 1.2x with a --color-accent-dim background wash.

Footer is a full-height cinematic reveal: as the user scrolls past the last content section, the footer rises from below like a curtain. The footer background is var(--color-surface-1), not var(--color-bg). It contains: the IIIT Delhi association line in mono, contact email, legal disclaimer, and a large background text "21 MAY 2025 / IIIT DELHI" at opacity 0.04 in var(--text-hero) scale.

No equal-width three-column card grids anywhere.
No standard hamburger-menu-right nav structure.

---

## Aurora Particle Canvas — Hero Background

A Three.js canvas fills the hero viewport behind all text. It renders a field of 4,000 particles in two color clusters: ~70% in var(--color-accent) (#4F7EFF) and ~30% in var(--color-accent-2) (#4FBFBF). Particles drift slowly upward and laterally, simulating the slow undulation of aurora borealis light bands.

```js
// Particle system setup
const scene = new THREE.Scene();
const camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(75, window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight, 0.1, 1000);
camera.position.z = 5;

const renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ canvas: document.querySelector('.aurora-canvas'), alpha: true });
renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2));
renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);

const particleCount = 4000;
const positions = new Float32Array(particleCount * 3);
const colors = new Float32Array(particleCount * 3);

for (let i = 0; i < particleCount; i++) {
  positions[i * 3]     = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 20;
  positions[i * 3 + 1] = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 10;
  positions[i * 3 + 2] = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 5;

  const isBlue = Math.random() < 0.70;
  colors[i * 3]     = isBlue ? 0.31 : 0.31;  // R: blue=0.31, teal=0.31
  colors[i * 3 + 1] = isBlue ? 0.49 : 0.75;  // G: blue=0.49, teal=0.75
  colors[i * 3 + 2] = isBlue ? 1.00 : 0.75;  // B: blue=1.00, teal=0.75
}

const geometry = new THREE.BufferGeometry();
geometry.setAttribute('position', new THREE.BufferAttribute(positions, 3));
geometry.setAttribute('color', new THREE.BufferAttribute(colors, 3));

const material = new THREE.PointsMaterial({ size: 0.02, vertexColors: true, transparent: true, opacity: 0.7 });
const particles = new THREE.Points(geometry, material);
scene.add(particles);

// Animate: slow undulation tied to scroll position via lenis
lenis.on('scroll', ({ progress }) => {
  particles.rotation.y = progress * 0.8;
  particles.rotation.x = progress * 0.2;
});

// Base idle animation
const clock = new THREE.Clock();
function animateParticles() {
  requestAnimationFrame(animateParticles);
  const elapsed = clock.getElapsedTime();
  particles.rotation.y += 0.0003;
  geometry.attributes.position.array.forEach((val, i) => {
    if (i % 3 === 1) geometry.attributes.position.array[i] += Math.sin(elapsed * 0.3 + i) * 0.0002;
  });
  geometry.attributes.position.needsUpdate = true;
  renderer.render(scene, camera);
}
animateParticles();
```

Canvas rules:
- transform: translateZ(0) on .aurora-canvas
- Canvas sits at z-index: 0. All hero text sits at z-index: 10.
- On mobile (width < 768px): reduce particleCount to 1200 and disable rotation.
- Canvas opacity fades to 0 as user scrolls past hero section: ScrollTrigger scrub tied to canvas opacity 1 to 0 over the first 30vh of scroll.

---

## Interaction Vocabulary

### 1. Custom Cursor

Built from scratch. No library. Native cursor hidden on * with cursor: none.

```html
<!-- Add once inside body -->
<div class="cursor-dot" aria-hidden="true"></div>
<div class="cursor-ring" aria-hidden="true"></div>
```

```css
* { cursor: none; }

.cursor-dot,
.cursor-ring {
  position: fixed;
  top: 0; left: 0;
  pointer-events: none;
  z-index: 9998;
  border-radius: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  will-change: transform;
}
.cursor-dot  { width: 5px; height: 5px; background: var(--color-accent); }
.cursor-ring {
  width: 32px; height: 32px;
  border: 1px solid var(--color-accent);
  opacity: 0.5;
  transition: border-color 0.2s;
}
```

```js
const cursorDot  = document.querySelector('.cursor-dot');
const cursorRing = document.querySelector('.cursor-ring');
const mouse = { x: 0, y: 0 };
const ringPos = { x: 0, y: 0 };

window.addEventListener('mousemove', (e) => {
  mouse.x = e.clientX; mouse.y = e.clientY;
  gsap.to(cursorDot, { x: mouse.x, y: mouse.y, duration: 0.06, ease: 'power4.out' });
});

gsap.ticker.add(() => {
  ringPos.x += (mouse.x - ringPos.x) * 0.09;
  ringPos.y += (mouse.y - ringPos.y) * 0.09;
  gsap.set(cursorRing, { x: ringPos.x, y: ringPos.y });
});

// Hover state: ring morphs to accent-filled circle with blend mode
document.querySelectorAll('a, button, .magnetic, .stage-card, .track-card').forEach(el => {
  el.addEventListener('mouseenter', () =>
    gsap.to(cursorRing, { scale: 3.2, opacity: 1, mixBlendMode: 'difference', duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' }));
  el.addEventListener('mouseleave', () =>
    gsap.to(cursorRing, { scale: 1, opacity: 0.5, mixBlendMode: 'normal', duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' }));
});

// On CTA hover: cursor ring fills solid, dot disappears
document.querySelectorAll('.cta-primary').forEach(el => {
  el.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {
    gsap.to(cursorRing, { scale: 4, backgroundColor: 'var(--color-accent)', opacity: 0.15, duration: 0.5, ease: 'expo.out' });
    gsap.to(cursorDot, { scale: 0, duration: 0.2, ease: 'power3.in' });
  });
  el.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
    gsap.to(cursorRing, { scale: 1, backgroundColor: 'transparent', opacity: 0.5, duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' });
    gsap.to(cursorDot, { scale: 1, duration: 0.3, ease: 'elastic.out(1, 0.5)' });
  });
});

// Click: dot scales up with elastic snap
document.addEventListener('mousedown', () =>
  gsap.to(cursorDot, { scale: 3, duration: 0.12, ease: 'power3.out' }));
document.addEventListener('mouseup', () =>
  gsap.to(cursorDot, { scale: 1, duration: 0.5, ease: 'elastic.out(1, 0.4)' }));
```

Magnetic pull on .magnetic elements (nav CTA, all primary buttons):
```js
document.querySelectorAll('.magnetic').forEach(el => {
  const xTo = gsap.quickTo(el, 'x', { duration: 0.55, ease: 'elastic.out(1, 0.4)' });
  const yTo = gsap.quickTo(el, 'y', { duration: 0.55, ease: 'elastic.out(1, 0.4)' });
  el.addEventListener('mousemove', (e) => {
    const r = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    xTo((e.clientX - r.left - r.width / 2) * 0.42);
    yTo((e.clientY - r.top - r.height / 2) * 0.42);
  });
  el.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { xTo(0); yTo(0); });
});
```

### 2. ScrollTrigger Scenes — Section by Section

Store every instance. Kill on Barba leave.

```js
const triggers = [];
barba.hooks.leave(() => { triggers.forEach(t => t.kill()); triggers.length = 0; });
```

Global scene rules:
- No two consecutive sections share the same animation pattern
- SplitText on all hero/display text. Char stagger for single words, word stagger for subheadings
- Char stagger: 0.04s, ease: "power4.out", y from 110%, parent overflow: hidden, padding-bottom: 0.15em
- Word stagger: 0.09s, ease: "expo.out", y from 50px, filter blur(6px) to blur(0px) simultaneously
- All SplitText parent containers: overflow: hidden, padding-bottom: 0.15em

---

#### Section 1 — Hero

ANIMATION PERSONALITY: The page does not fade in. It CRASHES in.

Page load sequence (not scroll-triggered — fires on DOMContentLoaded):

```js
const heroTl = gsap.timeline({ defaults: { ease: 'power4.out' } });

// 1. Background goes from opacity 0 to 1 instantly
heroTl.set('.aurora-canvas', { opacity: 0 })
      .to('.aurora-canvas', { opacity: 1, duration: 1.2, ease: 'expo.out' });

// 2. "AURORA" SplitText char reveal — chars fly in from y: 120%, stagger 0.05s
const heroSplit = new SplitText('.hero-title', { type: 'chars' });
heroSplit.chars.forEach(c => { c.style.overflow = 'hidden'; c.style.paddingBottom = '0.15em'; });
heroTl.from(heroSplit.chars, {
  y: '120%', duration: 1.0, stagger: 0.05, ease: 'power4.out'
}, 0.3);

// 3. Subheading "Global Hackathon 2025" fades in word by word
const subSplit = new SplitText('.hero-sub', { type: 'words' });
heroTl.from(subSplit.words, {
  y: 40, opacity: 0, filter: 'blur(6px)', duration: 0.8, stagger: 0.07, ease: 'expo.out'
}, 0.9);

// 4. "In association with IIIT Delhi" mono line — draws in left to right using clip-path
heroTl.from('.hero-institution', {
  clipPath: 'inset(0 100% 0 0)', duration: 0.9, ease: 'expo.out'
}, 1.2);

// 5. CTA button scales in from 0 with elastic bounce
heroTl.from('.cta-primary', {
  scale: 0.6, opacity: 0, duration: 0.7, ease: 'elastic.out(1, 0.6)'
}, 1.5);

// 6. Countdown ticker slides up from below
heroTl.from('.countdown-strip', {
  y: 80, opacity: 0, duration: 0.8, ease: 'expo.out'
}, 1.7);
```

PARALLAX: As user scrolls, hero content moves at different rates creating depth:
- Aurora canvas: scrub speed 0.2 (slowest — deepest layer)
- "AURORA" text: y moves -150px over 100vh of scroll at scrub: 1.2
- Subheading: y moves -80px over 100vh of scroll at scrub: 1.0
- CTA + institution line: y moves -40px over 100vh of scroll at scrub: 0.8

```js
// Parallax layers
const heroParallax = [
  { el: '.hero-title',     speed: 150 },
  { el: '.hero-sub',       speed: 80 },
  { el: '.hero-institution', speed: 40 },
];
heroParallax.forEach(({ el, speed }) => {
  triggers.push(ScrollTrigger.create({
    trigger: '.hero-section',
    start: 'top top',
    end: 'bottom top',
    scrub: 1.0,
    onUpdate: (self) => gsap.set(el, { y: -speed * self.progress })
  }));
});
```

---

#### Section 2 — Live Countdown Strip

A full-width horizontal bar just below the fold. Displays days:hours:minutes:seconds to the Round 1 deadline (17 April 2026).

Animation on scroll into view: each number block drops in from y: -40px, opacity 0, stagger 0.08s, ease: "expo.out", duration: 0.6s.

Micro-interaction: every time a digit changes (every second), the specific digit scales to 1.15 and snaps back with ease: "elastic.out(1, 0.6)" duration: 0.3s. This is the most frequent micro-interaction on the page — it must feel punchy and alive.

```js
function updateCountdown() {
  const target = new Date('2026-04-17T23:59:00');
  const now = new Date();
  const diff = target - now;

  const days    = Math.floor(diff / 86400000);
  const hours   = Math.floor((diff % 86400000) / 3600000);
  const minutes = Math.floor((diff % 3600000) / 60000);
  const seconds = Math.floor((diff % 60000) / 1000);

  const parts = { days, hours, minutes, seconds };
  Object.entries(parts).forEach(([key, val]) => {
    const el = document.querySelector(`.countdown-${key} .digit`);
    const formatted = String(val).padStart(2, '0');
    if (el.textContent !== formatted) {
      el.textContent = formatted;
      gsap.fromTo(el, { scale: 1.15 }, { scale: 1, duration: 0.3, ease: 'elastic.out(1, 0.6)' });
    }
  });
}
setInterval(updateCountdown, 1000);
updateCountdown();
```

Labels ("DAYS", "HOURS", "MINS", "SECS") in var(--font-mono) var(--text-xs) var(--color-muted) uppercase, letter-spacing +0.12em.

---

#### Section 3 — About / Mission

LAYOUT: Two columns. Left column: large display number "01." in var(--text-hero) at opacity 0.06, no interaction. Right column: all content.

ENTRY ANIMATION: Section enters with a clip-path reveal from bottom. The entire section content block animates from clip-path: inset(100% 0 0 0) to inset(0% 0 0 0) over 1.0s, ease: "expo.out", triggered when section is 20% into viewport.

After clip-path reveal, body copy paragraphs stagger in: each paragraph translates from y: 24px to 0, opacity 0 to 1, stagger 0.10s, duration 0.7s, ease: "expo.out".

SCROLL MICRO-INTERACTION: The large background number "01." scrubs from opacity 0.00 to 0.08 as the section enters, then back to 0.00 as it exits. This is tied to scrub: true.

Highlighted stat line ("7,000 to 15,000 participants / 50+ countries"): each number is wrapped in a span.stat-highlight. On scroll into view, each stat number counts up from 0 to its final value over 1.4s with ease: "power2.out". Use gsap.to on a proxy object with onUpdate to write to the DOM.

```js
triggers.push(ScrollTrigger.create({
  trigger: '.stat-7000',
  start: 'top 80%',
  once: true,
  onEnter: () => {
    const obj = { val: 0 };
    gsap.to(obj, {
      val: 7000, duration: 1.4, ease: 'power2.out',
      onUpdate: () => document.querySelector('.stat-7000').textContent =
        Math.round(obj.val).toLocaleString()
    });
  }
}));
// Repeat for 15000 and 50
```

---

#### Section 4 — Event Timeline (5 Stages) — Horizontal Pin

This is the structural centrepiece of the site. The section pins while the user scrolls 500vh. During those 500vh, a horizontal track of 5 stage cards translates left from 0 to -400vw.

```js
const tlTimeline = gsap.timeline();
tlTimeline.to('.stages-track', {
  x: () => -(document.querySelector('.stages-track').scrollWidth - window.innerWidth),
  ease: 'none'
});

triggers.push(ScrollTrigger.create({
  trigger: '.timeline-section',
  start: 'top top',
  end: () => `+=${document.querySelector('.stages-track').scrollWidth}`,
  scrub: 1.2,
  pin: true,
  animation: tlTimeline,
  anticipatePin: 1
}));
```

Each stage card specs:
- Width: 60vw minimum. Height: 80vh. Background: var(--color-surface-1).
- Stage number watermark: var(--text-hero) positioned top-right inside card, opacity 0.04, var(--color-text).
- Card entry (each card): as it enters the horizontal viewport, its content (title, description, date) reveals via SplitText word stagger, 0.08s stagger, ease: "expo.out", y from 30px, blur 4px to 0.
- Stage date badge: var(--font-mono) uppercase, background: var(--color-accent-dim), border: 1px solid var(--color-accent), border-radius: var(--radius-pill). Animates in from scale: 0.7 with ease: "elastic.out(1, 0.5)", duration: 0.5s.
- Active stage indicator: a thin 2px horizontal line at the bottom of each card draws in from left to right using DrawSVGPlugin over 0.6s ease: "expo.out" as that card enters viewport.

HOVER on each stage card:
```js
document.querySelectorAll('.stage-card').forEach(card => {
  card.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {
    gsap.to(card, { scale: 1.02, backgroundColor: 'var(--color-surface-2)', duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' });
    gsap.to(card.querySelector('.stage-line'), { scaleX: 1, duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' });
  });
  card.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
    gsap.to(card, { scale: 1, backgroundColor: 'var(--color-surface-1)', duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' });
  });
});
```

Progress indicator: a fixed thin horizontal bar at the top of the viewport fills from 0% to 100% width as the user scrubs through the timeline section. Color: var(--color-accent). This communicates "you are inside the timeline track" contextually.

---

#### Section 5 — Award Tracks

2x2 asymmetric grid (60/40 top row, 40/60 bottom row). Four track cards: Best Design, Best Innovation, Best Technology, Overall Winner.

ENTRY: Cards enter with staggered clip-path reveals. Top-left card: clip from left. Top-right card: clip from right. Bottom-left: clip from right. Bottom-right: clip from left. Stagger 0.15s between cards. Duration: 0.9s each, ease: "expo.out".

```js
const trackCards = document.querySelectorAll('.track-card');
const clipDirections = ['inset(0 100% 0 0)', 'inset(0 0 0 100%)', 'inset(0 0 0 100%)', 'inset(0 100% 0 0)'];
trackCards.forEach((card, i) => {
  triggers.push(ScrollTrigger.create({
    trigger: card,
    start: 'top 75%',
    once: true,
    onEnter: () => gsap.from(card, {
      clipPath: clipDirections[i], duration: 0.9, delay: i * 0.12, ease: 'expo.out'
    })
  }));
});
```

HOVER micro-interaction on each track card: a horizontal accent line at the top of the card draws in using DrawSVGPlugin from 0% to 100% on hover, retracts on mouseleave. Card title translates y: -4px. A faint var(--color-accent-dim) radial gradient bleeds from the top-left corner. All transitions: 0.35s ease: "expo.out".

Track names are eligible for sponsor naming (e.g. "Best Design — Sponsored by [Partner]"). The sponsor name is displayed in var(--font-mono) var(--text-xs) var(--color-muted) beneath the track name. If no sponsor, field is hidden.

---

#### Section 6 — Incubation Program

LAYOUT DISTINCTION: This section breaks the standard column layout. The content is placed in a single narrow column that is pushed to the right edge of the viewport — occupying only 50vw, flush right. The left 50vw is empty except for a large vertical "INCUBATION" text in var(--font-display) 800 weight, rotated 90 degrees counterclockwise, anchored to the left edge, opacity 0.04.

The --color-accent-2 (teal) makes its only body appearance here: a single 2px vertical line on the left edge of the content column, height 100% of section, color var(--color-accent-2). This line draws in from top to bottom using DrawSVGPlugin as the section enters, 1.2s ease: "expo.out".

ENTRY: Content items (4 bullet features of the incubation program) reveal with staggered y: 32px to 0, opacity 0 to 1, stagger 0.12s, duration 0.8s, ease: "expo.out".

Micro-interaction on each feature item: on hover, the feature's leading number (01, 02, 03, 04 in var(--font-mono)) scales to 1.4 and shifts color to var(--color-accent), duration 0.3s, ease: "expo.out". Item background goes to var(--color-surface-1). On mouseleave, reverses.

---

#### Section 7 — Judges / Mentors

This section is intentionally sparse. Judge cards are narrow portrait-aspect rectangles. They are arranged in a single horizontal row that overflows the viewport — users scroll horizontally by dragging or by a Lenis-driven horizontal scroll within this section (isolated, does not affect main scroll).

ENTRY: The row of cards enters from the right: the entire row translates from x: 15vw to 0, opacity 0 to 1, duration 1.0s, ease: "expo.out", triggered at section entry.

Each judge card hover: 3D tilt effect using gsap.to on rotateX and rotateY.
```js
document.querySelectorAll('.judge-card').forEach(card => {
  card.style.transformStyle = 'preserve-3d';
  card.style.perspective = '800px';
  card.addEventListener('mousemove', (e) => {
    const r = card.getBoundingClientRect();
    const xRot = ((e.clientY - r.top) / r.height - 0.5) * -14;
    const yRot = ((e.clientX - r.left) / r.width - 0.5) * 14;
    gsap.to(card, { rotateX: xRot, rotateY: yRot, scale: 1.04, duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out' });
  });
  card.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
    gsap.to(card, { rotateX: 0, rotateY: 0, scale: 1, duration: 0.5, ease: 'expo.out' });
  });
});
```

---

#### Section 8 — Partners Marquee

Infinite looping horizontal marquee. Partners logos scroll left continuously at 40px/s base speed.

```js
const marqueeTrack = document.querySelector('.marquee-track');
gsap.to(marqueeTrack, {
  x: () => -marqueeTrack.scrollWidth / 2,
  duration: () => marqueeTrack.scrollWidth / 2 / 40,
  ease: 'none',
  repeat: -1,
  modifiers: { x: gsap.utils.unitize(x => parseFloat(x) % (marqueeTrack.scrollWidth / 2)) }
});
```

HOVER on any logo: lenis.stop(), hovered logo scales 1.2, var(--color-accent-dim) background wash with border-radius var(--radius-md). All other logos decrease to opacity 0.4. On mouse leave: lenis.start(), all logos return to opacity 1, hovered logo scales to 1.

---

#### Section 9 — Register CTA

Full-viewport height. Background: var(--color-surface-1) — not var(--color-bg). This creates visual separation at depth.

The CTA headline is "SUBMIT YOUR IDEA" in var(--text-hero), weight 800. It is centered both axes. Letter-spacing -0.06em. The text is split into chars.

ENTRY: Chars enter from y: 100% stagger 0.04s ease: "power4.out" duration 0.9s as section enters viewport.

After headline enters: a horizontal animated border line draws from center outward (DrawSVGPlugin, 0% to 100% from center, duration 0.8s, ease: "expo.out"), then the CTA button scales in from 0.7 with ease: "elastic.out(1, 0.5)", duration: 0.7s.

HOVER on the primary CTA button: button background expands as a circular clip-path from center to full width. Background switches from transparent to var(--color-accent). Text color switches from var(--color-accent) to var(--color-bg). Duration: 0.4s ease: "expo.out".
```js
const ctaBtn = document.querySelector('.cta-primary');
ctaBtn.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => {
  gsap.to(ctaBtn, {
    '--clip-size': '200%', duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.out',
    backgroundColor: 'var(--color-accent)', color: 'var(--color-bg)'
  });
});
ctaBtn.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => {
  gsap.to(ctaBtn, {
    '--clip-size': '0%', duration: 0.4, ease: 'expo.in',
    backgroundColor: 'transparent', color: 'var(--color-accent)'
  });
});
```

---

#### Section 10 — Footer (Cinematic Reveal)

Footer rises from below as the last content section exits the viewport. This is a scroll-pinned reveal — the main content stays visible while the footer slides up beneath it.

```js
triggers.push(ScrollTrigger.create({
  trigger: '.footer',
  start: 'top bottom',
  end: 'top top',
  scrub: 1.5,
  onUpdate: (self) => gsap.set('.footer', { y: (1 - self.progress) * 100 + 'vh' })
}));
```

Footer contents:
- Large background text: "21 MAY 2026" in var(--text-hero), opacity 0.04, non-interactive
- "Aurora" wordmark flush left, mono
- "Organised by Aurora Organizing Committee in association with IIIT Delhi" in var(--text-sm) var(--color-muted)
- Contact email in var(--font-mono) var(--color-accent) with hover underline that draws via DrawSVGPlugin
- Legal disclaimer in var(--text-xs) var(--color-text-3)
- "50+ countries / 7,000-15,000 participants / May 2026" in var(--font-mono) var(--text-xs) flush right

---

### 3. Click / Tap Shockwave

Canvas overlay, global. Reads accent from CSS variable.

```js
const shockCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
shockCanvas.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;pointer-events:none;z-index:9997;';
document.body.appendChild(shockCanvas);
const shockCtx = shockCanvas.getContext('2d');

function resizeShock() { shockCanvas.width = window.innerWidth; shockCanvas.height = window.innerHeight; }
resizeShock();
window.addEventListener('resize', resizeShock);

document.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  const accentColor = getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--color-accent').trim();
  const obj = { p: 0 };
  gsap.to(obj, {
    p: 1, duration: 0.8, ease: 'expo.out',
    onUpdate() {
      const p = obj.p;
      shockCtx.clearRect(0, 0, shockCanvas.width, shockCanvas.height);
      shockCtx.beginPath();
      shockCtx.arc(e.clientX, e.clientY, p * 120, 0, Math.PI * 2);
      shockCtx.strokeStyle = accentColor;
      shockCtx.globalAlpha = (1 - p) * 0.6;
      shockCtx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      shockCtx.stroke();
      shockCtx.globalAlpha = 1;
    },
    onComplete: () => shockCtx.clearRect(0, 0, shockCanvas.width, shockCanvas.height)
  });
});
```

Shockwave rules: single ring, expands outward, fades on complete, no fill, no bounce. Fires on every click.

---

### 4. Page Transitions (Barba)

```html
<!-- Outside barba-wrapper, inside body -->
<div class="transition-overlay" aria-hidden="true"></div>
```

```css
.transition-overlay {
  position: fixed; inset: 0;
  background: var(--color-accent);
  transform: scaleY(0);
  transform-origin: bottom;
  z-index: 9990;
  pointer-events: none;
}
```

Transition personality: the overlay rises from the bottom of the viewport in 0.5s ease: "power4.inOut", filling the screen in electric blue before the new content enters. On enter, transform-origin switches to top and the overlay retracts upward in 0.6s ease: "expo.out". This is deliberately aggressive and cinematic.

```js
barba.init({
  transitions: [{
    name: 'aurora-transition',
    leave(data) {
      return gsap.timeline()
        .to(data.current.container, { opacity: 0, y: -16, duration: 0.3, ease: 'power3.in' })
        .to('.transition-overlay', { scaleY: 1, duration: 0.5, ease: 'power4.inOut', transformOrigin: 'bottom' }, '-=0.1');
    },
    enter(data) {
      lenis.scrollTo(0, { immediate: true });
      return gsap.timeline()
        .set('.transition-overlay', { transformOrigin: 'top' })
        .to('.transition-overlay', { scaleY: 0, duration: 0.6, ease: 'expo.out' })
        .from(data.next.container, { opacity: 0, y: 16, duration: 0.5, ease: 'power3.out' }, '-=0.35');
    },
    afterEnter() { ScrollTrigger.refresh(); }
  }]
});
```

---

## Additional Micro-Interactions — Full List

These are in addition to all animations specified above. Every interaction must feel instant and physical.

**Text selection:** Custom selection color: var(--color-accent-dim) background, var(--color-accent) text. Set via ::selection.

**Anchor links (smooth scroll to section):** On click, Lenis scrolls to target section. Section indicator on right edge animates immediately: current number scales down, new number scales up with elastic bounce.

**Nav "SUBMIT IDEA" button pulse:** Every 4 seconds, a faint var(--color-accent-glow) ring radiates outward from the button (same shockwave technique but smaller, opacity 0.3, radius 80px) to draw passive attention without being distracting.

**Image / card load skeleton:** Any card that contains async-loaded content (judge photos, partner logos) shows a skeleton shimmer: a var(--color-surface-2) background with a var(--color-border-2) shimmer stripe animating from left to right using a CSS background-position animation tied to a GSAP ticker, duration 1.2s, repeat -1.

**Form input focus (contact / registration fields):** On focus, the input's bottom border draws from left to right using a pseudo-element width transition: 0 to 100%, 0.35s ease: "expo.out". Color: var(--color-accent). Label floats upward: y from 0 to -20px, scale from 1 to 0.85, color shifts to var(--color-accent), duration 0.3s ease: "expo.out".

**Keyboard navigation:** All interactive elements have :focus-visible outlines using outline: 2px solid var(--color-accent), outline-offset: 4px. Never suppress focus outlines entirely.

**Mobile tap feedback:** On touchstart, the tapped element scales to 0.96, ease: "power2.out", duration 0.1s. On touchend, scales back to 1 with ease: "elastic.out(1, 0.5)", duration 0.4s.

**Section watermark numbers (01 through 09):** Each section's large background number (opacity 0.04) scrubs from opacity 0.00 to 0.06 as the section center reaches the viewport center, then back to 0.00. This makes the depth feel alive at every scroll position.

---

## Animation Rules — Always Enforced

- Every animation has a named ease. ease: "none" only for the horizontal timeline scrub.
- Timelines for 2+ moving parts. Standalone tweens for single micro-interactions only.
- will-change: transform on every animated element — add in CSS, not inline JS.
- transform: translateZ(0) on cursor elements, marquee track, aurora canvas, shockwave canvas.
- Duration minimum: 0.3s. Maximum without scroll-pin: 2.0s.
- Char stagger: 0.04s. Word stagger: 0.09s. Element stagger: 0.12s.
- All ScrollTrigger instances stored in triggers[] and killed on Barba leave.
- ScrollTrigger.normalizeScroll(true) set globally.
- gsap.config({ nullTargetWarn: false }) set globally.
- On mobile: disable 3D card tilt, reduce particle count to 1200, disable magnetic pull (touch devices only).
- Reduce motion: wrap all non-essential animations in a prefers-reduced-motion media query check. If reduced motion is preferred, disable parallax, particles, and stagger delays. Keep opacity reveals only.

---

## NEVER DO THIS

This section is a hard constraint list. The IDE agent treats every item as non-negotiable.

### Color violations
- Pure #000000 as any background — causes halation effect (white text appears to bleed and glow)
- Pure #FFFFFF as any background — clinically stark without intent
- Any accent with HSL saturation above 85% — visually vibrates against dark surfaces
- Pure primary colors as accent: #FF0000, #00FF00, #0000FF, #FFFF00, #FF00FF, #00FFFF — banned entirely
- Accent color applied to body copy — destroys 60-30-10 ratio
- var(--color-accent-2) teal used anywhere except the aurora canvas, the Incubation section divider line, and section 6 vertical line
- Two accents that are complementary (opposite on color wheel) in the same design
- Neon color as any text — bleeds into background, causes eye strain
- Neon on neon — zero effective contrast, unreadable
- Red and green together anywhere in the same view — colorblind failure and visual vibration
- Bright yellow (#FFEE00+) on white or near-white — contrast near zero
- Hot pink (#FF00AA+) on dark without desaturation — vibrates
- Two fully saturated colors (HSL saturation both above 80%) placed adjacent
- Any text with contrast ratio below 3:1 regardless of size
- Gradient text on any body copy — unreadable at small sizes
- Gradient text on any text under 32px — only permissible on large display text when contrast is verified
- Any shadow using pure black rgba(0,0,0,x) — use var(--color-bg) + opacity instead
- Hardcoded hex values anywhere outside the :root block
- Surface darker than the one below it in the elevation stack

### Layout and structure violations
- Equal-width three-column card grids
- Centered H1 + subheading + single CTA button hero layout — the hero is left-anchored, bleeds right
- Flat nav: logo left, links right, hamburger far right — the nav is wordmark + single magnetic CTA only
- Section headers that are just a bold centered H2 — every section has a structural decision
- Padding equal on all four sides of a section at top level
- The timeline section rendered as a vertical scrolling list — it is horizontal pin only

### Typography violations
- Font weight 500 on body copy
- Two serif families together
- Decorative or script font as body or display on dark background
- System fonts (Arial, Times, Georgia) as display typeface
- Any fixed px font size — fluid clamp only
- Body text container without max-width: 68ch
- "AURORA" hero text centered — it bleeds off the right edge, flush left

### Animation and code violations
- AOS, ScrollReveal, Animate.css, WOW.js, or any non-GSAP animation library
- ease: "none" on any visible motion except the horizontal timeline scrub
- opacity: 0 reveal without a paired y, x, scale, clip-path, or blur transform
- setTimeout for animation sequencing — use GSAP timeline position parameter
- transition: all — always target specific CSS properties
- Inline styles via element.style in JS — use GSAP or CSS custom properties only
- Generic loading spinner — if loader needed, it must be a custom aurora particle burst
- z-index above 9999 without an inline comment explaining why
- Lorem Ipsum — all copy must be real Aurora event content or marked [COPY TBD]
- Emojis anywhere — UI, comments, or copy
- Em-dashes in any copy or UI element — use commas, colons, or line breaks instead
- Three.js canvas visible after the user has scrolled past the hero section
- The marquee pausing without a corresponding lenis.stop() call
- The countdown digit animation firing on every render tick instead of only when the digit value changes
